tldr: Headlines fracture across four frames: record drone and missile attacks (SECURITY_DEFENSE, dominant), war crimes and accountability (CRIME_PUNISHMENT), ceasefire and prisoner-exchange diplomacy (POLITICAL), and European strategic repositioning (EXTERNAL_REGULATION). Nordic is the most isolated cluster (0.341), covering Stubb's shuttle diplomacy rather than the Kyiv drone barrage; Hungary_central (0.390) is the only bucket whose headline has no Ukraine content at all.
event_summary: Russia launched its heaviest wartime drone assault on Ukraine, killing at least 24 in Kyiv; Ukraine retaliated with strikes on the Ryazan oil refinery. A prisoner swap exchanged 205 combatants per side. The EU sanctioned Russian officials over the systematic deportation of Ukrainian children. A Ukrainian presidential adviser said a special tribunal on Russia could launch as early as 2027.

evidence.quote: Russia launches heaviest wartime drone assault on Ukraine
Death Toll In Kyiv Rises To 24 As Ukraine Hits Major Russian Refinery
Ukraine: EU sanctions Russians over 'systematic unlawful deportation'
expects special tribunal on Russia to launch as early as 2027
Russia, Ukraine swap 205 prisoners of war each
Kremlin says no clear roadmap to end Ukraine war despite Putin's words
Putin granted sweeping new powers to deploy troops abroad amid Ukraine War

isolation_top.note: Yle Finland leads with Stubb's shuttle diplomacy (frontline situation, Vilnius talks) — a European diplomatic-management frame distinct from the drone-barrage and accountability framings dominant in all other buckets.

Hungary's headline covers a domestic political story (president and Péter Magyar) with no Ukraine content — the only bucket in the corpus whose headline does not reference the war, explaining its extreme isolation from the corpus centroid.
The Punch leads with the prisoner swap as the headline event — a humanitarian-diplomacy frame that diverges from the military-assault framing dominant across the 13 SECURITY_DEFENSE buckets.

Italian outlet foregrounds the Ryazan refinery counterstrike and its implications for energy infrastructure — a subset of the SECURITY_DEFENSE frame but with a civilian-infrastructure emphasis absent from most other outlets.

Religious press leads with a Ukrainian mother's grief narrative ('My son was taken away by war') — a pure human-cost frame that contrasts with the operational military language dominant across the corpus.

silences.note: Hungary's headline concerns domestic politics (Péter Magyar / presidential photos) with no Ukraine content — a structural silence that reflects Hungary's political divergence from NATO-aligned Ukraine coverage.

single_outlet_findings.finding: The only headline in the corpus to frame the war through European macro-strategic repositioning ('How Europe Found Its Nerve') — an EXTERNAL_REGULATION register entirely absent from the 40 other articles which focus on battlefield events or legal accountability.

The only outlet to headline Ukrainian offensive success against Russia from a Russian perspective ('Ukrainian Drones Kill 4 and Set Ryazan Oil Refinery Ablaze') — an inversion of the victim/aggressor framing dominant in Western outlets covering the same event.

The only headline to foreground Polish complicity in US deportation practices as a Ukraine-war adjacency story — a legal accountability frame that folds a domestic Polish human rights critique into the Ukraine corpus.

bottom_line: Ukraine war headline coverage is dominated by the Kyiv drone barrage (13 of 26 buckets in SECURITY_DEFENSE) but fractures meaningfully into accountability (CRIME_PUNISHMENT) and diplomacy (POLITICAL) tracks. The extreme outlier is Hungary_central, the only bucket whose headline contains no Ukraine content whatsoever — its isolation (0.390) is a structural signal of political divergence, not a coverage gap. Nordic's shuttle-diplomacy focus and Foreign Affairs' 'How Europe Found Its Nerve' macro-frame are the two most analytically significant headline divergences, both pointing toward a European-agency narrative absent from the operational drone-strike coverage that dominates the rest of the corpus.
